idea-plugin.name: SQL to Java
idea-plugin.description:
SQL to Java DTO & DB Service Generator

Automatically generates Java DTO classes and database service classes from SQL CREATE TABLE statements.

DTO Generation

  • Paste SQL and instantly get Java classes with fields, constructors, getters, and toString()
  • Detects shared columns across tables and offers to generate an abstract superclass with subclasses
  • Generates Java records for simple tables (6 fields or fewer)
  • Automatically adds required imports (e.g. java.time.LocalDate)

DB Service Generation

  • Generate a full database service class with JDBC logic
  • Pick which CRUD operations you need: INSERT, SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE
  • Handles inheritance correctly — child classes get constructor args in the right order
  • Uses record accessors (field()) or class getters (getField()) as appropriate
  • Automatic LocalDate ↔ java.sql.Date conversion

International Support

Automatically converts non-ASCII characters to safe Java identifiers:

  • Scandinavian: æ/ø/å, ä/ö → ae/oe/aa
  • German: ä/ö/ü/ß → ae/oe/ue/ss
  • Icelandic: þ/ð → th/d
  • Romance languages: accented characters (é, ñ, ç, etc.) stripped to base letters

Save to Project

Generated files are saved directly to your project's source folder:

  • DTOs → src/dto/
  • DB services → src/db/

Usage

  1. Open the "SQL to Java" tool window from the sidebar
  2. Paste your CREATE TABLE statements
  3. Click "Convert to Java" for DTOs, or "Generate DB Service" for a service class
  4. Click "Save to Project" to create the files
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.2

  • New: Generate full DB service classes with CRUD operations (INSERT/SELECT/UPDATE/DELETE)
  • New: International character support (Norwegian, Danish, Swedish, German, Icelandic, Romance languages)
  • New: Files auto-save to correct folders based on package (src/dto, src/db)
  • Fix: Primary keys now stay on child classes instead of leaking into abstract parents
  • Fix: Record accessors (field()) vs class getters (getField()) handled correctly
  • Fix: LocalDate ↔ java.sql.Date conversion for JDBC compatibility
  • Refactor: Code split into focused modules (parser, detector, generator, file saver)
